_description: Desired improvements and unfinished work, derived from WINSWISHES.md and REFINEMENT_PLAN.md

wishes.description: Port CC11/CC12 handling from ReapNES_APU.jsfx to ReapNES_Console.jsfx
wishes.rationale: Console synth ignores per-frame volume/duty data, replacing ground-truth envelopes with generic ADSR. Fixes playback fidelity for all 1099 projects at once

wishes.description: Capture Contra APU trace for frame-level validation
wishes.rationale: Parser is at 96.6% volume accuracy but remaining gaps (decrescendo timing, UNKNOWN_SOUND_01, triangle precision) are blocked without trace data

wishes.description: Per-game ADSR tuning for keyboard play mode
wishes.rationale: Each game has a distinct envelope character (Mario=uniform 5-step, CV1=fast 4-step, Metroid=crescendo-decrescendo). Keyboard mode needs game-specific ADSR presets

wishes.description: Triangle linear counter precision to sub-frame accuracy
wishes.rationale: 195 sounding mismatches on CV1 labeled APPROXIMATE. Requires modeling APU 240Hz quarter-frame sequencer. Estimated 3-5 sessions

wishes.description: Implement EB vibrato command as pitch bend
wishes.rationale: EB is documented but unimplemented; silently skips bytes. TMNT and Gradius likely use vibrato and will produce corrupt output

wishes.description: Decode and render DPCM samples for Contra drums
wishes.rationale: Two physical samples (81-byte hi-hat, 593-byte snare) at $FC00 in bank 7. Currently white noise; real DPCM would transform drum sound

wishes.description: Expanded noise channel modeling with 16 rate presets and 2 modes (long/short)
wishes.rationale: Every percussion hit uses same white noise. Real NES noise has distinct timbres per period+mode combination for snare, hi-hat, kick, tom

wishes.description: Validate and port drum mapping table to Console synth
wishes.rationale: Console synth may handle noise differently from APU. If Console lacks the 35-entry drum mapping, drums will be wrong or silent

wishes.description: Super C as third Konami game to validate universal vs game-specific assumptions
wishes.rationale: Same driver family, mapper 2, 9/15 tracks already parsed with CV1 parser. Third data point reveals which assumptions are truly universal

wishes.description: Expansion audio support: MMC5 (CV3 US) then VRC6 (CV3 JP)
wishes.rationale: CV3 JP has the best Castlevania soundtrack and uses VRC6 (2 extra pulse + sawtooth). MMC5 first (trivial synth), VRC6 second

wishes.description: Non-Konami driver support starting with Capcom (Mega Man series)
wishes.rationale: Capcom driver covers 10-15 games from one parser. Second-best pipeline ROI after Konami completion

wishes.description: AddressResolver abstraction replacing hardcoded cpu_to_rom functions
wishes.rationale: Two hardcoded mapper functions (mapper 0 and mapper 2). Every new mapper requires a new function. ABC with Linear/BankSwitched/MMC3/MMC5 needed for scaling

wishes.description: Runtime manifest loading so parsers read addresses and configs from JSON at runtime
wishes.rationale: Manifests exist as reference docs but parsers hardcode addresses and byte counts. Manifest-driven parsing means new games need only JSON + ear-check, not code

wishes.description: Reference audio comparison tool (spectral diff between rendered WAV and NSF player output)
wishes.rationale: Semi-automates ear-checking by computing per-channel spectral difference and flagging frames where volume or pitch diverge above threshold

wishes.description: Deploy website to GitHub Pages (t3dy.github.io/ReapNES)
wishes.rationale: Jekyll site content exists (annotated source, methodology paper, research log, game matrix, Bach mashups) but was never deployed

wishes.description: Render WAVs for the 117 Bach fugue mashup REAPER projects
wishes.rationale: Fugues 1, 2, 5, 7 crossed with every CV1 and Contra palette exist as RPPs but have zero WAV files; likely interrupted by crash

wishes.description: Revisit CV2 extraction (7 tracks parsed despite 'dead end' label)
wishes.rationale: Focused listening session comparing WAVs to game would determine which tracks are correct and whether Fujio driver is closer to Maezawa than concluded

wishes.description: Commit all Antigravity session work to git
wishes.rationale: Scripts, song sets, Bach mashups, CV2/CV3 extractions, 36 docs, annotated source, trace JSONs, and session prompt are all untracked. One crash from gone

wishes.description: Improve note boundary detection to handle vibrato as pitch bend instead of spurious note splits
wishes.rationale: Some games use rapid period oscillation for vibrato that creates false note boundaries. Needs per-game analysis of ornamental vs real pitch changes

wishes.description: Contra volume envelope lookup tables for ROM-level fidelity
wishes.rationale: Contra uses 54 envelope lookup tables instead of CV1's parametric model. Tables identified in disassembly but not implemented
